name: Apple-Cheddar Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. You should have 12 to 15 thin slices of unpeeled red apple for this recipe. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
2. Cream the shortening and sugar together in a large bowl until well combined.
3. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
4. In a separate mixing bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Mix lightly.
5. Gradually stir the dry ingredients into the shortening mixture.
6. Alternately add the oats, cheddar cheese, and pecans to the batter, mixing well after each addition.
7. Gradually add the milk, stirring until all the ingredients are just moistened.
8. Grease 12 muffin cups with butter or cooking spray.
9. Fill each cup about two-thirds full with the batter.
10. Dip a few apple slices in melted butter and sprinkle lightly with cinnamon-sugar mixture.
11. Press one apple slice into the top of each muffin.
12. Bake for 25 minutes, or until golden brown.
